analysis_result: RAF_UP.V1_UP (MSigDB C6): NES = -0.7799, FDR q-val = 0.9706 (model reproduction). Reported values: NES = 0.9157, FDR = 0.8059. Both model and reported FDR values are far above significance threshold (>0.05), consistent with the paper's narrative that RAS/RAF/MEK pathway showed no significant enrichment in MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 (Supp Fig 5a-e).
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: Bulk RNA-seq (n=49 after QC) from PMC10172395. GSEA analysis of MPNST-G1 (n=8) vs MPNST-G2 (n=8) using MSigDB C6 oncogenic signatures. Target pathway: RAF_UP.V1_UP.

B. COHORT DEFINITION: Model used 6 vs 6 (not all G1/G2 samples); reported values likely from paper's full n=8 vs n=8 methylation-defined MPNST-G1/G2 groupings, or subset that passed RNA-seq QC.

C. VARIABLE CODING: Model used Welch t-statistic (G1-vs-G2) ranking on log-normalized counts; paper likely used limma/edgeR-style moderated t-statistic or pre-ranked GSEA with different ranking metric (Signal2Noise, log2FC, or moderated t).

D. COMPUTATION: gseapy.prerank with seed=42, 1000 permutations. Paper likely used GSEA Desktop (Broad Institute) with default phenotype-permutation GSEA using Signal2Noise metric, which is the standard for GSEA shown in Supp Fig 5c.

E. RESULT COMPARISON:
   Reported: FDR = 0.8059, NES = +0.9157
   Model:    FDR = 0.9706, NES = -0.7799
   FDR relative difference: (0.9706 - 0.8059) / 0.8059 = +20.4% (above 10% threshold)
   NES: sign flip (+0.92 vs -0.78); absolute difference |0.9157 - (-0.7799)| = 1.695

F. DIRECTIONAL INTERPRETATION: The NES sign difference is the most concerning discrepancy. A positive NES in the reported result indicates RAF_UP.V1_UP is enriched in MPNST-G1 (SHH-active subgroup), while the model's negative NES indicates enrichment in MPNST-G2. Both are non-significant (FDR >> 0.05), so the qualitative conclusion (no significant enrichment) is preserved, but the directional signal is opposite.

G. ROOT CAUSE: Most likely causes of the sign flip:
   1) Contrast direction: paper may have ranked G2-vs-G1 (so positive NES = up in G2) with RAF_UP.V1_UP showing modest positive NES in G2-direction interpreted as 'up in G1' axis after sign convention adjustment. Alternatively, the paper's GSEA tool (likely GSEA Desktop) uses phenotype labels where the first phenotype listed is the 'positive' direction; if paper listed MPNST-G1 first but paper text describes 'G1 vs G2' as 'G2 minus G1' (as in volcano plot Fig 1e: 'Beta Value Mean Difference (MPNST G2 minus MPNST G1)'), the sign convention could flip.
   2) Ranking metric: Welch t-statistic (model) vs Signal2Noise (GSEA default) vs moderated-t (limma) produce different rankings, especially in small-n settings (n=6-8 per group), which can reverse NES for weakly-enriched pathways.
   3) Sample inclusion: model used 6 vs 6, paper likely used 8 vs 8 (all high-grade MPNSTs by methylation). Two extra samples per arm in a 6-vs-6 setting is a ~33% change in sample size and can substantially alter rankings for non-significant pathways.
   4) Permutation strategy: phenotype permutation (GSEA Desktop default) vs gene-set permutation (gseapy.prerank default) yields different FDR estimates.
proposed_fix: (1) Run GSEA with phenotype-permutation (GSEA Desktop or gseapy.gsea, not prerank) using Signal2Noise or tTest metric on all 8 vs 8 MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 samples (not 6 vs 6). (2) Verify contrast direction matches paper convention: in this paper, Fig 1e shows 'G2 minus G1' as the primary contrast axis, so positive NES conventionally represents up-in-G2. Re-examine whether reported NES = +0.9157 means 'up in G1' or 'up in G2' in Supp Fig 5c legend. (3) Consider using the paper's likely pipeline: limma-voom ranking by moderated t-statistic, then fgsea with MSigDB C6 collection and nperm=10000. (4) If sign convention remains flipped after these corrections, inspect whether the paper used different gene symbol mapping (e.g., HGNC updates) or different C6 version (MSigDB version 6 vs 7 vs current).
